title: Manager, Academy – hybrid arrangement
description:
This role shall lead the Academy team to manage the business operations of the Academy, the development of dementia care training and consultancy services provided to external agencies.


Responsibilities

  • Lead the team to develop a business strategy for the marketing and delivery of training and consultancy programmes to professional caregivers and external agencies.
  • Engage relevant stakeholders, curriculum developers, trainers and subject matter experts to develop new dementia care training programmes and consultancy services which are person-centred, evidence-based, and relevant to the needs of learners.
  • Manage the Academy’s operational budget, monitor income and expenditure, and implement cost controls to ensure financial sustainability. Ensure the Academy achieves at least a breakeven position to support ongoing programmes, in compliance with organisational policies and governance requirements.
  • Develop business operations and systems for the Dementia Singapore Academy (DSA).
  • Ensure compliance to audit requirements of external certifying and funding bodies, such as the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), Skills Future Singapore (SSG) and Council for Private Education (CPE).
  • Prepare training and/or consultancy proposals for tender submissions, and to address the training and service needs of external partners, to enhance their quality of care for persons with dementia.
  • Submit proposals to funding agencies, such as AIC, SSG, National Silver Academy (NSA) and National Council of Service Services (NCSS).
  • Evaluate and monitor the effectiveness of DSA training programmes and consultancy services to ensure the quality.
  • Work with DSG Communications team to raise awareness of Academy training programmes and consultancy services, and publicise training courses and events.
  • Conduct training in own area of expertise as a dementia practitioner, where relevant.
  • Any other duties as assigned.


Requirements

  • Degree in Business, Marketing or Management.
  • At least 5 years of relevant work experience.
  • Competent in networking and multi-agency collaborations.
  • Experience or familiarity with SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) frameworks, including use of the Training Partner Gateway (TPG).
  • Possess strong administrative and project management skills.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
  • Previous experience in eldercare, healthcare or adult education sector is advantageous.
  • Certification in training/education/courseware development will be an added advantage.

postedCompany.description:
(1) Our Origins


Dementia Singapore (DSG) was formed in 1990 as Alzheimer’s Disease Association to better serve Singapore’s growing dementia community, increase awareness about dementia, and reduce the stigma surrounding the condition.


As Singapore’s leading Social Service Agency (SSA) in specialised dementia care, Dementia Singapore aims to advocate for the needs of people living with dementia and their families; empower the community through capability-building, knowledge and consultancy; and deliver quality, person-centred care innovations.


For more information, visit https://dementia.org.sg/



(2) Our Employee Value Proposition


Because We Care


“We are professionals committed to enhancing dementia care in Singapore.


We support each other in our common pursuit to inspire society to regard and respect persons living with dementia as individuals to lead purposeful and meaningful lives and create a more compassionate society.


Communication, openness and innovation are key traits within our family. We listen to each other’s feedback and suggestions, and work together collaboratively to make DSG a better place to work in.


We care for the well-being of our team so that we can provide the highest standard of care to our clients. This includes providing for career growth for all employees of DSG, while catering for good work-life harmony to allow for personal growth and development.”



(3) Hybrid Work Arrangement


As part of our Work-Life Harmony strategy, we adopted a hybrid work arrangement (subject to the nature of the job).


Flexi-Time


We have Staggered Timing for staff to choose their starting and ending work hours.

At DSG, we believe that achieving work-life balance will allow every valued staff of the DSG family to satisfy their professional and personal goals.



(4) Diversity, Equity & Inclusion at the Heart of Our Culture

At Dementia Singapore, diversity, equity, and inclusion are not add-ons, they are integral to who we are and how we work. We embrace a culture where every team member feels valued, respected, and empowered to contribute meaningfully. 


We are committed to providing equal opportunities for career development, and we believe that diverse perspectives lead to better outcomes for the people we serve.
postedCompany.name: DEMENTIA SINGAPORE LTD.
